pageMeta.title: AI Agents for Travel Agency Support and Sales
pageMeta.description: DocsBot helps travel agencies answer traveler questions, qualify trip inquiries, and retrieve policy details with source-grounded AI agents.

hero.eyebrow: Source-grounded AI agents for travel advisors and tour teams
hero.headline: Help travelers decide, book, and prepare with DocsBot AI agents
hero.subheadline: DocsBot lets travel agencies build AI support and internal knowledge agents trained on your itineraries, supplier notes, destination guides, booking policies, FAQs, and service standards. Give travelers faster answers while your advisors focus on complex planning, personalization, and high-value bookings.

proofBullets.text: Answer destination, trip-prep, cancellation, documentation, and service questions from approved agency content.
Collect trip preferences, budget, dates, traveler counts, and follow-up details before routing qualified leads to advisors.
Use configured Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflows for approved live lookups, summaries, ticket creation, and handoffs.
Support multilingual traveler conversations in more than 100 languages, with escalation when a human advisor should step in.

hero.primaryCta: Build your travel agency AI agent

businessClarity.whatDocsBotDoes: DocsBot helps a travel agency turn owned knowledge into practical AI agents. Train agents on your website, PDFs, itineraries, destination pages, supplier guides, trip policies, SOPs, and internal notes so customers and staff can retrieve accurate, approved answers instead of searching scattered documents or repeating the same explanations.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Advisors need fast access to trip inclusions, destination rules, cancellation terms, supplier details, and traveler preferences while keeping the personal touch that wins bookings.

bullets.text: Retrieve policy, destination, and package details without digging through PDFs, spreadsheets, or email threads.
Summarize traveler requirements before consultation calls so advisors can spend more time designing the right trip.
Draft source-grounded responses for common questions, then let the advisor review sensitive pricing or commitment details.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Owners need consistent service quality across advisors, channels, and peak travel seasons while protecting margins on repetitive support work.

bullets.text: Deflect repetitive questions about deposits, documents, insurance options, itinerary timing, and what is included.
Use analytics and conversation review to spot repeated traveler confusion and improve trip pages, FAQs, and advisor scripts.
Support scalable inquiry handling in an industry where U.S. travel agencies reached an estimated $46.4 billion market size in 2025.

whoItHelps.role: Support, reservations, and group travel teams
whoItHelps.roleContext: Reservations teams handle changes, document questions, rooming-list details, payment reminders, and supplier handoffs that can quickly overwhelm shared inboxes.

bullets.text: Create structured intake flows for change requests, accessibility needs, passport questions, and group traveler updates.
Route complex, urgent, or supplier-dependent issues to the right person with conversation context attached.
Help staff respond consistently during disruption-heavy periods when delays, baggage, oversales, complaints, and TSA service issues affect travelers.

evidenceSources.metric: $46.4 billion U.S. travel agency market size in 2025
evidenceSources.claim: IBISWorld estimates the U.S. travel agencies industry market size at $46.4 billion in 2025, showing the scale of agency demand and competition.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Travel Agencies in the US - Market Research Report (2015-2030)

evidenceSources.metric: Travel agents remain a distinct U.S. occupation despite online self-booking
evidenceSources.claim: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics notes that online travel resources can limit demand for travel agents, making advisor expertise and service efficiency important differentiators.

evidenceSources.metric: DOT tracks flight delays, baggage, oversales, complaints, and TSA customer service reports
evidenceSources.claim: The Department of Transportation’s Air Travel Consumer Report covers categories travel agencies often need to explain during traveler support and disruption handling.

evidenceSources.metric: Group travel generates $319 billion in annual U.S. economic impact
evidenceSources.claim: U.S. Travel Association reports that group travel for business events, leisure tours, sports, and live entertainment generates $319 billion in annual economic impact and supports over 3 million jobs.

evidenceSources.metric: U.S. travel agency air ticket sales reached $95.3 billion in 2023
evidenceSources.claim: Airlines Reporting Corp. reported $95.3 billion in U.S. travel agency sales in 2023, based on monthly sales data from 10,362 U.S. retail, corporate, satellite, and online agency locations.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: U.S. Travel Agency Sales Total $95.3 Billion in 2023

agentUseCases.name: Trip inquiry qualification and advisor routing
agentUseCases.problem: Travelers often ask broad questions before sharing the details an advisor needs: dates, destination flexibility, budget, group size, travel style, passport status, and urgency.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: A DocsBot presales agent answers initial trip questions from your approved content, asks structured follow-up questions, captures traveler preferences, summarizes the opportunity, and routes the inquiry to the right advisor or group travel specialist through a customer-configured form, CRM, email, helpdesk, or workflow.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With approved Skills, APIs, connectors, or scheduling workflows, the agent can collect consultation intent, create a lead record, request a meeting time, and attach the conversation summary for human follow-up.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Can you help us plan a two-week Italy honeymoon under $9,000?
We need a spring break trip for six people with flights, resort, and airport transfers.
Do you handle corporate incentive travel for 80 attendees?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Advisors receive cleaner, better-qualified inquiries and spend less time asking basic intake questions before a planning call.

agentUseCases.name: Booking policy and trip-prep answer agent
agentUseCases.problem: Agencies repeat the same answers about deposits, final payment dates, cancellation rules, travel insurance, passport timing, visa guidance, room categories, transfers, and baggage expectations.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot trains on your FAQs, terms, trip pages, supplier notes, destination briefs, and preparation documents to give travelers source-grounded answers in your agency’s voice. When a request is complex, sensitive, or requires supplier confirmation, the agent can prepare an escalation summary for a human advisor.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Customer-configured live lookups can retrieve approved booking status, due dates, or document checklist context when your systems expose that data through Skills, APIs, connectors, MCP Connectors, or workflow automations.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: When is my final payment due for the Greece trip?
What documents do I need before traveling to Costa Rica?
What is included in the tour price, and what is paid locally?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Travelers get faster self-service answers while your team keeps control over policy interpretation, refunds, pricing, and supplier commitments.

agentUseCases.name: Disruption and change-request triage
agentUseCases.problem: Flight delays, schedule changes, baggage issues, traveler complaints, and urgent itinerary questions can flood an agency’s phone and inbox during peak disruption periods.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: A DocsBot support agent collects the traveler’s booking reference, trip date, carrier, destination, issue type, and urgency; answers general agency process questions from approved content; and routes the case to the right support queue with a concise summary and next-step recommendation.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: When connected to approved systems, AI Actions can create a ticket, notify a team channel, update allowed fields, or attach a disruption summary for the human handling the case.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: My flight was canceled and I’m supposed to start my tour tomorrow. What should I do?
My bag is missing after the connection. Who should I contact?
Can I change the traveler name on this booking?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Urgent issues reach the right human faster, while routine process explanations are handled consistently. DOT reports cover delays, mishandled baggage, oversales, complaints, and TSA service data that frequently shape traveler questions.

agentUseCases.name: Group travel rooming-list and attendee support
agentUseCases.problem: Group trips generate repetitive questions from travelers, hosts, chaperones, and coordinators about deadlines, forms, inclusions, room types, payment milestones, and special requests.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can answer from group trip microsite content, PDFs, payment policies, traveler instructions, packing notes, and FAQ pages. It can collect missing information, summarize special requests, and route exceptions to operations staff.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With approved workflows, the agent can submit structured traveler updates, create tasks for coordinators, or trigger notifications when a traveler asks about accessibility, dietary needs, or deadline exceptions.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: How do I submit my passport details for the choir trip?
Can my family request connecting rooms?
What is the deadline to choose the optional excursion?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Group coordinators reduce repetitive back-and-forth and keep travelers aligned around the same source of truth in a segment with major U.S. economic impact.

agentUseCases.name: Internal supplier and destination knowledge assistant
agentUseCases.problem: Agency knowledge often lives across supplier PDFs, training decks, advisor notes, destination webinars, spreadsheets, and past conversations, making it hard for newer staff to answer confidently.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot acts as a private internal knowledge agent trained on your approved supplier guides, SOPs, preferred partner notes, brand standards, sales playbooks, and destination content. Staff can ask natural-language questions and receive source-grounded answers with citations to the underlying material.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Teams can access the assistant through private chat experiences, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or configured internal workflows depending on how the agency deploys DocsBot.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Which preferred partners support small-ship Alaska cruises?
What is our process for post-booking insurance follow-up?
Where is the latest advisor script for destination wedding inquiries?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: New and experienced advisors spend less time searching and deliver more consistent guidance across destinations, suppliers, and service teams.

agentUseCases.name: Content gap and traveler question analytics
agentUseCases.problem: If travelers repeatedly ask the same unanswered questions, the agency may not know which trip pages, FAQs, or advisor materials need improvement.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot provides analytics, logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery so teams can see what customers and staff are trying to learn.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Operations leaders can review recurring questions, update knowledge sources, and refine agent instructions so future answers stay aligned with the latest agency content.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Which destination pages generate the most unanswered questions?
What are travelers asking before booking but not finding on our site?
Which support topics should we add to our pre-departure guide?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Agency content improves over time, reducing repeat questions and giving advisors better materials to support sales conversations.

customerFacingAgent.headline: Give travelers immediate help without losing the advisor relationship
customerFacingAgent.intro: DocsBot can power a website or trip-page AI support agent that answers from your approved travel content, collects the details your team needs, and escalates when a traveler needs judgment, urgency, supplier confirmation, or personalized planning.

requests.request: “Which honeymoon package is best for Greece in September?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Answer from approved package pages and destination guides, ask follow-up questions about budget, travel dates, departure city, interests, and desired pace, then summarize the lead for an advisor.
requests.optionalAction: Submit a custom lead form or send a qualified inquiry summary to the agency’s configured sales workflow.

requests.request: “What happens if I cancel after final payment?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Retrieve the agency’s cancellation terms, supplier-specific notes, and travel protection content, explain the general policy clearly, and recommend human review for booking-specific or refund-sensitive decisions.
requests.optionalAction: Create a support ticket with booking context if the traveler wants an advisor to review their exact reservation.

requests.request: “Do I need a passport, visa, or vaccination for this trip?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Point travelers to the agency’s approved trip-prep guidance and disclaimers, explain what information the agency provides, and escalate destination-specific or high-impact compliance questions when needed.
requests.optionalAction: Collect departure date, nationality, destination, and traveler status so an advisor can follow up with appropriate guidance.

requests.request: “Can someone call me about a family trip to Japan?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Ask for trip dates, traveler ages, interests, budget range, preferred contact method, and timing, then provide a concise handoff to the right specialist.
requests.optionalAction: Use an approved scheduling workflow or handoff process to request a consultation time.

requests.request: “Can you explain my itinerary day by day?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Use the agency’s itinerary content to summarize the trip sequence, inclusions, meeting points, optional activities, and preparation notes in traveler-friendly language.
requests.optionalAction: Escalate itinerary discrepancies or missing supplier confirmations to the operations team.

requests.request: “Do you support travelers in Spanish or French?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Respond in the traveler’s language when configured and answer from the same approved knowledge sources, preserving consistency across multilingual conversations.
requests.optionalAction: Route multilingual inquiries to the appropriate advisor if the agency wants language-specific follow-up.

internalAgent.headline: Make agency knowledge easier for advisors and operations teams to use
internalAgent.intro: DocsBot can serve as a private internal knowledge agent for travel advisors, support staff, sales coordinators, and operations teams who need quick answers from approved company knowledge, not scattered files or memory.

workflows.name: Advisor sales desk assistant
workflows.problem: Advisors need fast answers while building quotes, comparing suppliers, and responding to traveler objections.
workflows.agentWorkflow: Train DocsBot on service catalogs, supplier notes, sales playbooks, proposal templates, past approved FAQs, and objection-handling guidance so advisors can ask for talking points, policy summaries, or package comparisons.
workflows.optionalAction: Draft a follow-up email or proposal note for human review using approved workflow automation.
workflows.outcome: Faster, more consistent sales follow-up without handing final pricing, legal terms, or commitments to the agent.

workflows.name: Operations SOP retrieval
workflows.problem: Staff may not remember the exact process for traveler changes, final documents, rooming lists, payment reminders, emergency contacts, or supplier handoffs.
workflows.agentWorkflow: DocsBot retrieves step-by-step procedures from SOPs, internal wikis, PDFs, and training materials, then gives staff concise instructions with source-grounded context.
workflows.optionalAction: Create a task, route a request, or notify a team channel when a configured workflow is authorized.
workflows.outcome: Teams follow the same process more reliably across advisors, branches, and seasonal staff.

workflows.name: New advisor onboarding
workflows.problem: New advisors need to learn agency positioning, preferred partners, booking procedures, escalation paths, and destination basics before they can work independently.
workflows.agentWorkflow: A private DocsBot agent answers onboarding questions from training decks, brand guidelines, HR policies, destination resources, and internal procedures so new hires can learn while working.
workflows.optionalAction: Surface repeated onboarding questions so managers can improve training materials.
workflows.outcome: New staff ramp faster and ask managers fewer repetitive questions.

workflows.name: Support ticket summarization and routing
workflows.problem: Long traveler email threads can hide key details like trip dates, booking status, requested change, prior promises, and urgency.
workflows.agentWorkflow: When connected through customer-configured helpdesk workflows, DocsBot can summarize the thread, identify missing details, draft a response from approved sources, and recommend the right escalation path.
workflows.optionalAction: Route the ticket, prepare a handoff, or draft a response for review in the agency’s configured support process.
workflows.outcome: Support staff spend less time reconstructing context and more time resolving traveler needs.

trustClaims: Travel industry teams already use DocsBot to help communities access information that supports booking conversion.
DocsBot has proven it can handle high-volume support, including over 30,000 inquiries in one month in the Sony NURO case study.
Teams can train DocsBot on owned content libraries so answers reflect the knowledge the business approves.
trustAndProof.whyTheseReviewsFit: Travel agencies need dependable self-service, advisor enablement, and content-grounded answers across many destinations, policies, and booking stages. DocsBot proof spans travel training, high-volume customer support, and intuitive training on owned content libraries, giving agency leaders confidence that AI agents can support both travelers and staff.

schemaHints.serviceType: AI support agents and internal knowledge automation for travel agencies

faq.question: Can DocsBot answer traveler questions without giving unapproved advice about visas, passports, refunds, or insurance?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot is designed to answer from your approved agency content and escalate when a question requires judgment, supplier confirmation, legal interpretation, or booking-specific review. You can train it on trip-prep guides, policy disclaimers, supplier notes, and FAQs so it provides consistent guidance while keeping advisors responsible for high-impact decisions.

faq.question: How would an AI agent help us qualify vacation inquiries before an advisor gets involved?
faq.answer: DocsBot can ask structured intake questions such as destination, dates, traveler count, budget range, departure city, travel style, flexibility, passport status, and preferred follow-up method. It can then summarize the opportunity and route it through a configured form, CRM workflow, email, helpdesk, or scheduling process so advisors start with cleaner context.

faq.question: Will this replace our advisors or just reduce repetitive pre-booking and support questions?
faq.answer: DocsBot is best positioned as an assistant for repetitive, content-grounded work: answering trip inclusions, payment deadlines, document reminders, itinerary details, and service questions. Advisors still handle relationship building, complex planning, supplier negotiation, pricing judgment, exceptions, and final recommendations—the work that differentiates agencies from self-service booking sites.

faq.question: What travel agency content should we train the agent on first?
faq.answer: Start with the content that causes the most repeated questions: trip pages, itinerary PDFs, cancellation terms, deposit and final payment policies, travel protection explainers, destination guides, pre-departure checklists, group travel instructions, and advisor SOPs. Then use DocsBot analytics and knowledge-gap discovery to see which traveler questions need better source material.

faq.question: Can DocsBot handle urgent disruption questions during flight delays, cancellations, or baggage issues?
faq.answer: DocsBot can triage disruption requests by collecting booking reference, trip date, carrier, destination, issue type, and urgency; answering general process questions from approved content; and routing the case to the right human with a concise summary. That matters because DOT Air Travel Consumer Reports track delay, baggage, oversales, complaint, and TSA service categories that often drive traveler support volume.
faq.role: Support, reservations, and group travel teams

faq.question: How do we prevent the agent from promising refunds, upgrades, inventory, or supplier exceptions?
faq.answer: You can configure the agent’s instructions, source scope, escalation rules, and workflow boundaries so it does not make commitments outside approved content. For sensitive topics—refund eligibility, name changes, room upgrades, supplier waivers, air schedule changes, or travel insurance claims—it can explain general policy and prepare a handoff for human review.
faq.role: Support, reservations, and group travel teams

faq.question: Can the agent support group travel without creating more operational cleanup for coordinators?
faq.answer: Yes, if it is trained on the specific group’s microsite, forms, rooming instructions, payment schedule, trip inclusions, deadlines, and exception rules. DocsBot can answer attendee questions, collect missing information, summarize special requests, and route accessibility, dietary, deadline, or rooming exceptions to staff instead of leaving them buried in email threads.
faq.role: Support, reservations, and group travel teams

faq.question: What ROI should an agency owner look for beyond simple chat volume?
faq.answer: Track repetitive questions deflected, qualified inquiries created, advisor time saved before consultation calls, faster response times during peak seasons, fewer incomplete intake forms, and content gaps fixed. For larger agencies, the opportunity is meaningful: IBISWorld estimates the U.S. travel agency market at $46.4 billion in 2025, so efficiency and service consistency can affect competitiveness.

faq.question: Can DocsBot work for both leisure travel and corporate or incentive group inquiries?
faq.answer: Yes. You can train different agents or knowledge scopes around leisure packages, luxury planning, destination weddings, student groups, corporate incentive travel, tours, or business travel. Group travel is a large opportunity area: U.S. Travel Association reports $319 billion in annual U.S. economic impact from group travel across business events, leisure tours, sports, and live entertainment.

faq.question: How does DocsBot fit with our booking systems, CRM, forms, helpdesk, or scheduling tools?
faq.answer: DocsBot can use customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, MCP Connectors, forms, webhooks, or workflow automations for approved actions such as lead forwarding, ticket creation, task routing, meeting requests, live lookups, and summary handoffs. It should be scoped to the data and actions you authorize rather than treated as an unrestricted booking-system user.

faq.question: Can the agent look up a traveler’s final payment date or booking status?
faq.answer: It can when your agency exposes that data through an approved Skill, API, connector, metadata binding, or workflow. A common setup is to let the agent retrieve limited booking context, answer from approved policy content, and escalate when the traveler asks for a change, refund, supplier exception, or anything requiring human judgment.
faq.role: Support, reservations, and group travel teams

faq.question: How do we keep answers current when supplier policies, trip details, or destination requirements change?
faq.answer: DocsBot should be connected to the content your team already treats as authoritative—current trip pages, approved PDFs, SOPs, supplier notes, and pre-departure guides. When content changes, update or resync those sources, review conversation logs for outdated answers, and use knowledge-gap reporting to identify materials that need revision.

faq.question: Can our advisors use DocsBot internally instead of searching supplier PDFs and shared drives?
faq.answer: Yes. A private internal knowledge agent can be trained on supplier guides, SOPs, destination training, preferred partner notes, sales playbooks, proposal templates, and onboarding materials. Advisors can ask natural-language questions and receive source-grounded answers, helping newer and experienced staff work from the same approved knowledge base.

faq.question: What reporting will tell us whether travelers are getting stuck before they book?
faq.answer: DocsBot provides analytics, logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery. Agency leaders can review repeated questions by destination, package, payment policy, document requirement, or group trip to improve public pages, advisor scripts, intake forms, and pre-departure materials.

faq.question: Can the agent answer in Spanish, French, or other languages without creating inconsistent policy explanations?
faq.answer: DocsBot can support conversations in more than 100 languages while answering from the same approved knowledge sources. That means multilingual travelers can receive faster help, but the agency can still rely on one controlled set of policies, trip guides, FAQs, and escalation rules rather than separate unsupported translations.
faq.role: Support, reservations, and group travel teams

faq.question: What security controls matter if we train the agent on internal SOPs, supplier contracts, or traveler conversations?
faq.answer: DocsBot offers SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, granular access controls, role-based permissions, and no use of customer data to train public models. For connected actions, Skills can be configured with secrets, metadata bindings, network policy, logging, and customer-defined controls.

faq.question: How should we handle PCI, payment, passport, and other sensitive traveler information?
faq.answer: Use the agent to explain approved payment processes, deadlines, and document requirements—not to collect unnecessary sensitive data in open chat. Configure intake fields, escalation rules, and workflow boundaries carefully, keep credentials out of conversations, and route payment or identity-document handling through the secure systems your agency already approves.
faq.role: Support, reservations, and group travel teams

faq.question: How long does implementation usually take for a travel agency knowledge agent?
faq.answer: The fastest path is a focused first release: choose one audience, one or two high-volume workflows, and a clean set of approved sources such as FAQs, trip pages, terms, and pre-departure documents. After launch, expand into lead qualification, internal advisor support, group travel workflows, and live lookups as your content and governance mature.

faq.question: Do we need a perfect knowledge base before launching?
faq.answer: No, but you do need approved source material for the topics you want the agent to answer. Many agencies begin with imperfect but authoritative content, restrict the agent to those sources, review unanswered questions, and improve documents over time. DocsBot’s analytics and knowledge-gap discovery help turn real traveler questions into a content roadmap.

faq.question: How can we test answer quality before putting the agent on our website?
faq.answer: Build a test set from real traveler emails, consultation questions, group travel FAQs, cancellation scenarios, and advisor objections. Have advisors review whether responses cite the right source, stay inside policy, avoid unsupported promises, and escalate correctly. Start privately or on a limited page before expanding to broader traveler-facing use.

faq.question: How does DocsBot compare with a generic website chatbot or live chat widget?
faq.answer: A generic chat widget often depends on scripted flows or a human operator. DocsBot is built around source-grounded AI agents trained on your approved knowledge, with escalation, analytics, internal knowledge use cases, and optional bounded actions through configured Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflows. It is better suited to policy-heavy travel questions than a basic chat box.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help us respond faster during seasonal peaks without hiring temporary support?
faq.answer: DocsBot can reduce pressure during peak inquiry periods by answering repeated questions about documents, inclusions, deposits, transfers, room types, deadlines, and trip preparation. It can also summarize and route complex cases so staff spend less time gathering context. This is especially useful in a high-volume sector where ARC reported $95.3 billion in U.S. travel agency air ticket sales in 2023.

faq.question: What happens when a traveler asks something the agent cannot answer from our sources?
faq.answer: The agent should say it does not have enough approved information, ask clarifying questions if helpful, and escalate to the right advisor or support queue with the conversation summary. That behavior is important for destination rules, supplier exceptions, accessibility needs, medical concerns, insurance claims, and anything that could materially affect the traveler’s trip.
faq.role: Support, reservations, and group travel teams

faq.question: Can we use DocsBot to improve advisor onboarding and training?
faq.answer: Yes. A private DocsBot agent can answer onboarding questions from brand standards, SOPs, supplier training, destination resources, escalation paths, and sales playbooks. Managers can also review repeated employee questions to identify where training decks, checklists, or process documentation need to be clarified.

faq.question: Who should own the AI agent after launch: marketing, operations, advisors, or IT?
faq.answer: Most agencies need shared ownership. Operations should own policy accuracy and escalation rules, advisors should review sales and destination answers, marketing should improve public content based on question trends, and IT or an admin should manage access, data connections, and workflow permissions. Clear ownership prevents the agent from becoming an unmanaged content channel.

faq.question: Can the agent draft responses for advisors without sending them automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can draft source-grounded replies, itinerary explanations, policy summaries, sales follow-ups, or support responses for human review. That is useful when the answer needs the agency’s voice but still requires an advisor to verify pricing, supplier status, traveler context, tone, or any commitment before it goes out.
